replies.author: @TheRealRealMClovin
replies.text:
I was okay with the darth vader bleeding thing.
Though I don’t know how in legends they explained the mystery of sith’s red crystals.

But how they do bleeding now of like Acolyte that any lightsaber just turns red the more people you kill with anger.
So freaking stupid!

replies.author: @DGas-rg1pk
replies.text: ​@TheRealRealMClovin In the Expanded Universe the red crystals used by Sith were synthetic. They were chosen by Sith because they output more power than the naturally occurring Kyber crystal. which is so much more in line with the Sith credo of maximizing power above all else. "Bleeding" is effort for no functional return, really OOC for the faction. Plus, other existing Sith magic follows that rule of being in service of power, which  makes this especially anomalous. People just saw crystals adn though, "Oh, they aren't mystical enough. They're just components fro construction of lightsabers? Let's give them souls because they're in line with the force." Meanwhile, the Dark Side is one half of the force, so by that logic, shouldn't there be naturally occurring crystals that contain dark side souls? And thus we get into a quagmire of contradiction that never needed to exist by just keeping the crystals crystals.

replies.author: @dhaburuk6494
replies.text:
​@TheRealRealMClovinIt makes some sense - at least to me - that a Dark Force-user could taint a crystal, but not quickly & not absolutely. Seeing that fallen-Jedi dude in Jedi:Survivor do something to his yellow crystal in a mere moment to make it orange is silly to me. I think it would take years.

Jedi don't select a crystal as much as they match-up with one, so they have some resonance that could make it easier to taint the crystal, but living a life in the Light would also make it harder to taint if a Jedi were to Fall & keep the same crystal.

Maybe this is just me, but this new fangled 'bleeding' thing smells like low-effort writing if it can be done by having what I see as a big tantrum in the Force.

author: @KryyssTV
text:
Unfortunately what is needed for AI to handle the combat is a model trained specifically for recreating character movement and then something like a LoRa that applies that character to the sequence. Sort of like setting up an animation rig and then applying a mesh. Its not very reliable unfortunately.

This is what many of the AI haters don't realise. AI is so dumb and primitive currently that trying to coax anything useful from it takes hours of manual adjustments BY A PERSON who understands how the AI interprets inputs.

It's no joke to say that wrangling AI is almost like needing to  understand a programming language. It takes skill, patience and experience.

author: @Bloodgod40
text: I can't really get on board with a video about Vader bleeding his crystal red, it does nothing for me, because the whole idea of bleeding crystals came from Disney. It's tainted from the outset. I prefer the pre-Disney EU explanation for why Sith wield red sabers - they use synthetic ones, and a side effect of how they are made is that they are red. I like that because it ties in with the different ethos of the Jedi and Sith. The Jedi go with the flow, letting the Force guide them to a secret cave where they can find a crystal for them. The Sith, who are all about imposing their own will and order on the universe, just make their own.

Synthetic crystals make them basic and feel less important imo. I get it though, your opinion is pretty popular for a reason.

author: @Berniebrew82
text:
Hey Theory, I know you were frustrated the other day about how these reaction videos get more from your viewers than other vids, but just wanted to remind you it’s not necessarily a bad thing.
It shows we still consider you the godfather of star wars fandom on YT, and when it comes to these new AI videos we want to experience them with you because you’ve been such a core part of our own star wars fandom. I genuinely want to see your reaction because it’s just like your comic reads come to life. It almost brings out the excitement I had as a kid to watch these live action movies for the first time. I cant get enough of it and hope you continue doing these reactions so we can all share the experience (and also put more pressure on Disney lol). Much love Theory! You’re the best man

author: @kylocarmis648
text: Bleeding cystals is dumb IMO. That is all.

replies.author: @CrispyCrusader0.o
replies.text: It's pretty cool imo. Why is it dumb?

replies.author: @kylocarmis648
replies.text:
​​​​​@CrispyCrusader0.oI'm just a puritan from the Lucas era where colors of crystals represented different types of Jedi and a color of crystal was chosen accordingly. So, Sith just chose a red or violet crystal due to what it represents. So Sith using a violet crystal is now broken, which causes issues for the Old Republic where that was primarily seen and that irritates me as I'm a massive KOTOR guy. But like I said, I'm old school.

It also doesn't help that Disney made it dumb by having crystal colors change mid-combat (I'm looking at you Osha). By that logic, Anakin's should have changed after slaying Younglings. And yes, I realize they are supposed to physically touch the crystal to "bleed" it, so they gave Osha a shitty hilt that had the crystal sticking out of it to get around that, but again...I think stuff like that is just dumb. So the continuity and "lore" behind it is broken.

But again, this is just my opinion, but I'm certainly not alone with said opinion.

Regarding that toy channel it's possible they BOUGHT views AND subs. That is a very real thing here on youtube. The number of VIEWS and subs to their number of videos does NOT make sense at all.

Edit: Forgot to mention seeing and hearing Tarkin was unexpected and I thought they nailed him down perfectly. I dunno if you noticed that when Vader and Palpatine were fighting in his office the window kept switching from day to night. It's like the AI couldn't keep things straight.
